Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Renal function shows mildly elevated serum creatinine, suggesting possible early renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), which may indicate underlying renal or urinary tract pathology. Blood glucose and HbA1c are low, but not critically so. No evidence of urinary infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). Recommend further evaluation of renal function and possible nephrotic or tubular disorder; clinical correlation and follow-up advised.
